system.description: A rough wooden club. The PHB treats it as a simple blunt weapon that can also be thrown.
A simple light mace with a balanced head for one-handed strikes.
A heavier mace for crushing armor and bone.
A spiked mace head that deals both blunt and piercing damage.
A short utility blade favored as a backup or throwing weapon.
A one-handed spear that may also be thrown.
A double weapon. The current automation uses the primary 1d6 end and notes the off-hand end in its properties.

automation.notes: Double weapon. Off-hand attack uses the second 1d6 end and two-weapon rules.

system.description: A two-handed spear suitable for melee or throwing.
A weighted throwing dart, effectively a very small javelin.
A simple sling. Stones are weaker than bullets, but the base profile assumes sling bullets.

automation.notes: Primary damage assumes sling bullets. Stones are a weaker improvised ammunition option.

system.description: A light flexible spear designed for throwing. In melee it is nonproficient by the PHB.

automation.notes: PHB notes melee use is nonproficient and suffers a -4 attack penalty.

system.description: Metal gloves that let unarmed strikes deal normal rather than subdual damage.
A fitted ring of heavy metal that adds crushing force to punches.
A punch dagger that drives the blade with the force of the wielder's strike.
A spiked gauntlet counts as an armed attack and cannot be disarmed normally.
A heavy curved dagger with an inward cutting edge.
A lighter axe balanced specifically for throwing.
Three claw-like blades fixed to the back of the hand.
A small war hammer light enough to throw.
A one-handed axe suited for close combat.
A short sickle-like martial weapon.
Two short sticks linked by rope or chain.
A light war pick focused on armor-piercing strikes.
A weighted club used for knocking targets out instead of killing them.

automation.notes: Deals subdual damage only.

system.description: A short piercing martial weapon.
A compact sword ideal for close fighting or off-hand use.
A standard martial axe with strong chopping power.
A broad-bladed sword optimized for hacking cuts.
A lighter flail that excels at tripping and disarming.

automation.notes: Supports trip and disarm bonuses that are not yet automated.

system.description: A straight, reliable martial sword.
A heavier war pick designed to punch through armor.
A light thrusting sword suited to precise fencing.
A curved blade with an especially keen edge.
A three-pronged spear that can be thrown or braced.
A heavy hammer built for war.
A chain with wicked barbs. It can attack at reach or adjacent and grants trip and disarm options.

automation.notes: Reach, adjacent attacks, trip, disarm, and finesse interactions need dedicated handling.

system.description: A two-handed curved blade, essentially a large war scimitar.
A two-handed flail with strong trip and disarm utility.

automation.notes: Trip and disarm bonuses are not yet automated.

system.description: A polearm that attacks at reach but not adjacent.

automation.notes: Reach-only targeting is not yet enforced.

system.description: A massive two-handed axe.
A huge club reinforced for war.
A massive two-handed blade.
A reach polearm with a hooked blade for tripping.

automation.notes: Reach-only attacks and trip handling need dedicated support.

system.description: A polearm with axe head, hook, and spike for trip and brace actions.

automation.notes: Trip and brace options are not yet automated.

system.description: A long spear used at reach and especially deadly against chargers.

automation.notes: Reach-only attacks and brace vs. charge are not yet automated.

system.description: A reach polearm with a strong disarming hook.

automation.notes: Reach-only attacks and disarm bonuses are not yet automated.

system.description: A war scythe focused on devastating critical hits.
PHB special case: a Medium wielder with Strength 17+ and Weapon Focus (bastard sword) may use it one-handed.

automation.notes: One-handed use depends on the PHB special Strength 17+ and Weapon Focus rule.

system.description: A massive maul that smashes armor and bone.
A fighting net used to entangle foes rather than deal damage.

automation.notes: Requires ranged touch handling, entangled condition, rope control, and refolding rules.

system.description: A whip deals subdual damage and has several special combat rules.

automation.notes: Whip damage restrictions, trip, disarm, and ranged-melee special handling need dedicated automation.

system.description: Thrown stars. The PHB allows up to three thrown per attack.

automation.notes: PHB allows up to three shuriken per attack and ignores Strength to damage.

system.description: A martial shortbow.

automation.notes: Uses arrows as ammunition.

system.description: A recurved composite shortbow. Special Strength pull variants are not yet modeled.

automation.notes: Uses arrows. Strength-rated variants are not yet modeled separately.

system.description: A full-size war bow for long-ranged shooting.

automation.notes: Uses arrows as ammunition.

system.description: A recurved composite longbow. Strength pull variants are a future automation layer.

automation.notes: Uses arrows. Strength-rated variants are not yet modeled separately.

system.description: System convenience weapon for monks and martial artists. Not a PHB table entry.
A retained legacy entry outside the PHB weapon table.
Retained legacy crossbow entry. Crossbows are not on the PHB Chapter 7 weapon table used for this audit.
Retained legacy hand crossbow entry outside the PHB Chapter 7 table.
